text.title: fine-tuning-openai-eval Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for fine-tuning-openai-eval as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Evaluating only on the training distribution.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A fine-tuned model memorizes training patterns. Eval on a test set drawn from the same distribution as training overestimates production quality. Evaluate on real traffic samples or distribution-shifted examples to measure actual generalization.

failure-mode.description: Using the fine-tuned model itself as the eval judge.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The fine-tuned model has learned biases from training data — it will score its own output style higher. Always use a different model (typically gpt-4o) as the judge to avoid circular evaluation.

failure-mode.description: Skipping human review before production deployment.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Automated metrics catch metric-level regressions (accuracy, format, win rate). They miss subjective failures: inappropriate tone for brand context, subtle safety violations, outputs that are technically correct but user-hostile. Human checkpoint is mandatory before routing live traffic.
